commit_message:
[Transform] disable index call optimization (#77341)

disable optimization of index calls introduced in #75839 as it can create wrong results.
See #77329 for follow up

relates #77329
fixes #77310

commit_message:
Fix failing non snapshot ci build (#77335)

After moving test-die-with-dignity to an external test module (see #77136) we need
to ensure the javaRestTests are only triggered in snapshot builds as they fail
on non snapshot builds.

Fixes #77326

commit_message:
Take into account the latest valid snapshot index metadata id (#77412)

Closes #77343

issue_title: Vector tile format does not handle fields with dots in name
commit_message:
Support for aggregation names with dots in vector tiles (#77477)

Change the way we generate the pipeline aggregation by adding the metric ( in our case '.value') so we 
don't fail in aggregations with names containing dots.

commit_message: Awaitsfix for #77408

issue_title: Index requests with incorrect types can cause confusing errors
issue_body:
Applies to all released 7x versions of elasticsearch.

1) Create an index (with no type) that contains a `foo` field mapped as `keyword`
2) Send an index request with type `bar` that contains a string `foo` field
3) The index action will notice that there is no `bar` type, so create an empty mapper which then dynamically assigns the `foo` field a text mapper
4) The indexing code will try and merge this new dynamic mapper into the root, and get an error because `foo` is mapped both as text and keyword: `mapping update rejected by primary - java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: mapper [foo] cannot be changed from type [keyword] to [text]`

This error is confusing because the actual cause of the problem is the incorrect type.  We should detect the type mismatch earlier and return a better error.
commit_message:
Fail early if an index request has the wrong type (#77417)

If an index request contains a type that is different to the type defined on the
index, we fail the request with an error. However, this check happens fairly
late on in the process, and if the index request has generated dynamic updates
that conflict with the existing mappings on the index then merge errors will
get thrown instead. This is confusing to users, as it is not at all obvious why
these conflicting mappings have been generated.

This commit adds a check for valid types early in the indexing process, so that
requests with invalid types will be failed with a more informative error message.

Fixes #77411

commit_message:
Fix shell retry generator for shells other than bash (#77422)

Closes #77414.

The generated shell logic in ShellRetry doesn't work in more basic shells, so instead
of using the {1..10} construct, expand the sequence.

commit_message:
Remove custom logging tests (#77476)

configurations. Users should update their config to latest version when
upgrading.

closes #77430

commit_message:
Re-enable shutdown stalled test (#106498)

NodeShutdownIT.testStalledShardMigrationProperlyDetected has been muted
for a couple years. It apparently reproduced when the failure first
started, but no longer reproduces on main. This commit re-enables the
test and closes the test issue. We can open a new issue with any
subsequent failure.

closes #77456

commit_message:
Wait for shard migration status update on shutdown (#106497)

The shutdown integration tests test scenarios across multiple nodes.
When checking if a shard is moved off a node that is shutting down, the
shard migration status may not yet have been updated. This commit adds a
busy wait to ensure the status has time to update before failing the
test.

closes #77488

issue_title: _terms_enum not supporting fields by alias for non-super user
issue_body:
**Elasticsearch version** (`bin/elasticsearch --version`): 7.14.1 /7.15.0

**OS version** (`uname -a` if on a Unix-like system): darwin_x86_64

**Description of the problem including expected versus actual behavior**: _terms_enum stopped supporting fields by alias.

This bug is the result of an SDH and discuss post - https://discuss.elastic.co/t/kibana-filter-window/283522

**Steps to reproduce**:
 1.  Create an alias to index pattern
 2. Create a user with a role with all privileges to the alias from the first step.
 3. Login with that user and try to create a filter - filter values are not getting populated. 

This works in 7.13.4 and in 7.15.0 - this works if I change autocomplete:valueSuggestionMethod to terms_agg from terms_enum (default) in Kibana advanced settings.

Here is the Kibana bug -  https://github.com/elastic/kibana/issues/111642

A problem occurred evaluating root project 'elasticsearch-carrot2'.

issue_title: High level REST client POM file dependencies are incorrect
issue_body:
The POM file for our high level REST client isn't being generated correctly. This seems to come down to us building a shadow jar.

```
  <dependencies>
    <dependency>
      <groupId>org.elasticsearch</groupId>
      <artifactId>server</artifactId>
      <version>8.0.0-SNAPSHOT</version>
      <scope>runtime</scope>
    </dependency>
    <dependency>
      <groupId>org.elasticsearch.client</groupId>
      <artifactId>rest</artifactId>
      <version>8.0.0-SNAPSHOT</version>
      <scope>runtime</scope>
    </dependency>
  </dependencies>
```

There's two issues here. First is that these are `runtime` scoped, but in reality, these dependencies are leaked through the high level client API so they should be `compile`. I think this is just a limitation of the shadow plugin as by default it simply uses `runtime` for anything added to the `shadow` configuration.

Second is that the `artifactId`s are wrong. I believe this comes down to us renaming the publication artifacts for these projects. The `:server` project becomes `elasticsearch` and `rest` becomes `elasticsearch-rest-client`.

So yeah, we can't actually resolve these dependencies, and if we _were_ able to, they wouldn't be added to the compile classpath like they should.

commit_message:
Force typed keys in the HLRC get async search (#78992)

This change ensures that the HLRC can parse the search response
that the get async search returns.
The aggregations must be typed by keys but the param to enable this
mode was missing.

Closes #77608

commit_message:
Avoid early release of local forking requests (#77641)

Today we protect against releasing a request from a remote node until
its handler has completed, but we do not have the same protection for
requests from the local node. This commit adds the missing refcounting.

Relates #77407
Closes #77634

issue_body: I'm not sure what's up with #76740, but some test failures seem to have snuck in. I'm going to disable the failing tests and link the here.
commit_message:
fix composite aggregation tests failing after #76740 (#77691) (#77779)

Follow up of #77650.

Looks like two issues caused occasional failures:

missingOrder has not been considered in InternalComposite.InternalBucket#compareKey
reverseMul has been ignored in one case in OrdinalValuesSource#compareInternal
The first problem has clearly been introduced by #76740 but the second point is more interesting. As far as I understand, this might have caused problems before but I couldn't find any according test failures in Gradle before yesterday.

In order to preclude further rare test failures I've run the whole CompositeAggregatorTests 3000 times and all tests turned green.
# Conflicts:
#	server/src/main/java/org/elasticsearch/search/aggregations/bucket/composite/InternalComposite.java

commit_message:
Reenable MlDistributedFailureIT

Rreenable testClusterWithTwoMlNodes_RunsDatafeed_GivenOriginalNodeGoesDown
 after Lucene 8.10 upgrade

Closes #77655

issue_title: Routing partition size is not final
issue_body: The `index.routing_partition_size` setting isn't `Properties.Final` but it sure *feels* like it'd be dangerous to change at any time, even if the index is closed.
commit_message:
[7.x] Make `index.routing_partition_size` final (#79556) (#79616)

Changing the property value after the index has been created actually doesn't change the partition size value in IndexMetadata. Let's make sure that this value is final to to prevent confusion.

References #77669

issue_title: ignore_malformed = true doesn't work for geo_shape fields
commit_message:
Remove InvalidShapeException from GeoPolygonDecomposer (#77613)

 fixes a bug where InvalidShapeException are not properly handle so even if the mappings defined with
ignore_malformed set to true, the exception leaks and the setting is ignored

commit_message:
Fix version error in cardinalit profile tests (#77715)

I fixed an incorrect `skip` in the cardinality profiling tests
yesterday. But! I fixed them incorrectly. Sadness. The new profiling was
indeed added in 7.10, but an element of the profiling `post_collect`
didn't exist until 7.12`. This updates the skip to be after 7.12 - we
don't get any tests for the rest of the profiling, but those versions
are pretty old now anyways.

Closes #77708, #77709, #77710

commit_message:
Fix version error in cardinalit profile tests (#77715)

I fixed an incorrect `skip` in the cardinality profiling tests
yesterday. But! I fixed them incorrectly. Sadness. The new profiling was
indeed added in 7.10, but an element of the profiling `post_collect`
didn't exist until 7.12`. This updates the skip to be after 7.12 - we
don't get any tests for the rest of the profiling, but those versions
are pretty old now anyways.

Closes #77708, #77709, #77710

commit_message:
Fix testConcurrentConnectsAndDisconnects (#83098)

As mentioned in #77729 each call to `connectToNode` temporarily holds a
reference to the connection it just opened, so we must wait for all the
`connectToNode` calls to complete before we can assert that the node is
no longer connected. However sometimes we run the connection validator
in the background, which means the temporary reference lives for longer
than the call to `connectToNode`.

This commit tracks the lifetime of the temporary reference into the
validator. In fact we don't need to track the calls to `connectToNode`,
it's enough to wait for every call to the validator to complete.

Closes #77728

Fix rate agg with custom `_doc_count` (#79346)

When running a rate aggregation without setting the field parameter, the result is computed based on the bucket doc_count.

This PR adds support for a custom _doc_count field.

Closes #77734

commit_message:
Notify stubbable transport behaviors on clear (#77774)

If `MockTransportService#addUnresponsiveRule` adds a rule that drops
requests to a node then we must notify the rule on removal so that the
requests don't leak. Today we notify rules when removing them from a
specific address but not if clearing all rules. This commit addresses
that.

Closes #77751

commit_message:
Adjust SearchableSnapshotsBlobStoreCacheIntegTests.testBlobStoreCache (#77758)

This test sometimes fails (#77753) when it tries to refresh the
snapshot blob cache index while it wasn't created at all due to
the test index being completely empty with 0 documents indexed.

Having an empty index in this test does not make much sense
because the index will contain no segments and therefore no
segments file to cache in the system index.

This commit adjusts the test to always index at least
some docs.

Closes #77753

**Elasticsearch version** (`bin/elasticsearch --version`):
7.14.1

**JVM version** (`java -version`):
bundled with .deb
```

**OS version** (`uname -a` if on a Unix-like system):
Linux elk00001 4.19.0-17-amd64 #1 SMP Debian 4.19.194-3 (2021-07-18) x86_64 GNU/Linux

**Description of the problem including expected versus actual behavior**:
The cluster.routing.allocation.exclude._name setting directive of PUT /_cluster/settings accepts non-string values. I am able to submit an array in to this setting which is not honoured, nor is an error raised. The documentation specifies a comma separated string must be provided. https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/current/modules-cluster.html#cluster-routing-settings

**Steps to reproduce**:
```
curl -u 'elastic:<redacted>' -X PUT "localhost:9200/_cluster/settings?pretty" -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d'
{
  "transient" : {
    "cluster.routing.allocation.exclude._name" : [ "elk00001","elk00002","elk00003"]
  }
}'
response:
{
  "acknowledged" : true,
  "persistent" : { },
  "transient" : {
    "cluster" : {
      "routing" : {
        "allocation" : {
          "exclude" : {
            "_name" : [
              "elk00001",
              "elk00002",
              "elk00003"
            ]
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

root@elk00001:~# curl -u 'elastic:<redacted>' localhost:9200/_cluster/settings?pretty
{
  "persistent" : { },
  "transient" : {
    "cluster" : {
      "routing" : {
        "allocation" : {
          "exclude" : {
            "_name" : [
              "elk00001",
              "elk00002",
              "elk00003"
            ]
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

N/A

commit_message:
Wait for shard index folder to be cleaned up (#80341)

The tests testCreateAndRestoreSearchableSnapshot and 
testCreateAndRestorePartialSearchableSnapshot both 
failed once when asserting the shard folders using 
assertShardFolders(index, true).

The failures occurred when the original index is first closed 
(not deleted) and mounted again under the same name (so 
it will be restored as a searchable snapshot index on top of 
the existing shard files). The SearchableSnapshotDirectory 
implementation takes care to clean up the shard files on disk 
using SearchableSnapshotDirectory.cleanExistingRegularShardFiles() 
and the tests verify that the shard index folder is indeed deleted 
from disk on all nodes but sometime fail because the folder 
is still present.

I wasn't able to reproduce but I think that the closing of the 
original index + the creation of the .snapshot-blob-cache 
index trigger some shard relocations that are cancelled by 
the subsequent mount/restore, leaving some files on disk 
that should be cleaned up but maybe not immediately.

This commit changes the tests to assertBusy() when 
verifying the shard folders and also adds more logging 
information in case waiting for the 
assertShardFolders(index, true) is not enough.

Closes #77831

issue_body:
Multiple tests failed with `process was found dead while waiting for ports files`. The underlying issue is shown in the test cluster log file: The node crashed when starting up because it didn't read any valid content from the `/proc/self/cgroup` file. I wonder whether there is a subtle racing condition somwhere setting up the test.

commit_message:
Handle empty /proc/self/cgroup file (#78659)

Older versions of the Linux kernel, e.g. 4.1.12 which
is found in OEL-6, can sometimes have empty cgroup file
causing a test assertion. This change removes the
assert and handles the empty file like a non-existent
file.

Closes #77833

commit_message:
Don't apply the spotless plugin to non-applicable projects (#80679) (#80680)

This is an attempt at resolving, or at least reducing the prevalence of
#77837. A more robust solution is probably required, but this should
help reduce this problem since we now apply spotless to about 90 fewer
subprojects.

issue_title: [DOCS] Error resolution of "disk usage exceeded flood-stage watermark"
issue_body:
### error
@jrodewig I'd like to recommend the following text for resolving the common user encountered [error](https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/blob/master/server/src/main/java/org/elasticsearch/cluster/metadata/IndexMetadata.java#L91-L93) (core+variations)

core

> disk usage exceeded flood-stage watermark, index has read-only-allow-delete block. Affected indices [x,y]

variations

> HTTP 429 {'type':'cluster_block_exception','reason':'index [x] blocked by: [TOO_MANY_REQUESTS/12/disk usage exceeded flood-stage watermark, index has read-only-allow-delete block]'}

> [x] policy [y] for index [z] failed on step [{'phase':'hot','action':'rollover','name':'check-rollover-ready'}]. Moving to ERROR step org.elasticsearch.cluster.block.ClusterBlockException: index [z] blocked by: [TOO_MANY_REQUESTS/12/disk usage exceeded flood-stage watermark, index has read-only-allow-delete block];

### response

I consistently offer this general copy/pasted advice to encountering users:

> Cloud and on-prem Elasticsearch clusters use [disk-based shard allocation](https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/current/modules-cluster.html#disk-based-shard-allocation) to manage where they distribute index's shards within the cluster. This benefits spreading the load, preventing data overflow/overwrites, etc. Part of this feature is watermark levels (summarized w/default levels): 
> 
> - 75% UI disk allocation warns red colored but no system action
> - 85% low disk stops allocating shards
> - 90% high disk moves shards off node
> - 95% flood stage sets index to read-only (auto-unsets after)
> 
> Your current deployment status can be seen via API ([GET _cat/allocation?v=true](https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/current/cat-allocation.html)). Kibana may go down at the low disk (85%) mark and is practically guaranteed to go down at the flood stage (95%), because its system indices (`.kibana*`) will not be able to write updates. You may choose to temporarily override disk watermarks to be able to recover your cluster. To override:
> 
> ```
> PUT /_cluster/settings
> { "transient": {
>    "cluster.routing.allocation.disk.watermark.low": "90%",
>    "cluster.routing.allocation.disk.watermark.high": "95%",
>    "cluster.routing.allocation.disk.watermark.flood_stage": "97%" }}
> 
> PUT _all/_settings
> { "index.blocks.read_only_allow_delete": null }
> ```
> 
> After, to reset
> 
> ```
> PUT /_cluster/settings 
> { "transient": { 
>    "cluster.routing.allocation.disk.watermark.low": null,
>    "cluster.routing.allocation.disk.watermark.high": null,
>    "cluster.routing.allocation.disk.watermark.flood_stage": null }}
> ```
> 
> We recommend users scale out their cluster ([similar to hitting max heap](https://www.elastic.co/blog/managing-and-troubleshooting-elasticsearch-memory)), but alternatives/supplements include:
> 
> - decrease shards via deleting unnecessary/old data or turning off replicas for data you don't care to lose 
> - [use ILM](https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/current/getting-started-index-lifecycle-management.html) to put data into [warm/cold storage](https://www.elastic.co/blog/hot-warm-architecture) so you can [shrink](https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/current/ilm-shrink.html) it

### request

I'd like to recommend/request this be added to Elasticsearch documentation. I couldn't find a smooth way to incorporate it under [Disk-based shard allocation settings](https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/current/modules-cluster.html#disk-based-shard-allocation) though that seems to be the closet topic-wise. 🙏🏼

[DOCS] Troubleshoot the flood-stage watermark error (#78519)

Adds troubleshooting steps for the flood-stage watermark error.

Closes #77906.

commit_message:
Preventing serialization errors in the nodes stats API (#90319)

Preventing serialization errors in the nodes stats API, and adding logging to the ingest counter code
so that we can find the root cause of the problem in the future.

issue_title: Cancelling retrieval of multiple `SnapshotInfo` does not always complete the task
issue_body:
**Elasticsearch version** (`bin/elasticsearch --version`): `master`, `7.14.1`

**Description of the problem including expected versus actual behavior**:

If the retrieval of multiple `SnapshotInfo` blobs is cancelled (e.g. you make a get-snapshots call and then close the connection) then we clear the queue and call `context.onFailure` here:

https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/blob/c0a967e952fcc9ad3b107509aa3e96179ba455d3/server/src/main/java/org/elasticsearch/repositories/blobstore/BlobStoreRepository.java#L1516-L1520

However the context will only complete the outer listener once it's received `snapshotIds.size()` notifications (unless `abortOnFailure` is set) so by clearing the queue we leak the task.

FWIW we also log at `WARN` for every worker that encounters an exception, including cancellation. Cancellation is to be expected, we shouldn't log anything above `DEBUG`.
commit_message:
Fix Get Snapshots Request Cancellation with ignore_unavailable=true (#78004)

Short-circuit the failure method when cancelled just like in the fail fast case.
Also, remove the special case handling that asserts but swallows exceptions in production
for when ignoring unavailable to not swallow the task cancellation exception.

closes #77980

issue_title: Transport handshake starts before TLS handshake completes
issue_body: When opening a connection to a remote node we establish a collection of TCP channels and once they're all open we send an `internal:tcp/handshake` to the remote node with a 30-second timeout. However today we don't wait for the TLS handshake to complete before considering a channel to be open. This means if the TLS handshake fails (e.g. it times out after 10s) then we report the `internal:tcp/handshake` as failing due to the channel closing, rather than reporting a more useful error about the TLS handshake failure. We should wait for the TLS handshake to complete before considering each channel to be open.
commit_message:
Fix fransport handshake starting before tls handshake completes (#90534)

Implements waiting for the TLS handshake to complete before triggering the connected
callback and thus the transport handshake.
Had to make copies of two transport test cases with slight but not trivial alterations
to force a tls handshake before doing either a timeout or a conneciton reset.
Will dry that up in a follow-up as it's a bit a noisy refactoring.

closes #77999

commit_message:
Fix SimpleBlobStoreCacheService (#78176)

The SearchableSnapshotDirectory contained some logic 
to ignore cached blob from .snapshot-blob-cache whose 
range do not match the expected content. This logic was 
introduced when we changed the size of cached blobs.

A recent change #77611 moved that logic from the 
directory to the BlobStoreCacheService. But the logic 
to ignore wasn't replicated to other blob store cache 
service implementations used in tests like 
SimpleBlobStoreCacheService. This triggers failures 
like #78150 were a cached blob that should be treated 
as a cache miss was not ignored anymore, causing 
some IOE.

This pull request changes BlobStoreCacheService 
and SimpleBlobStoreCacheService so that only it 
only overrides the get/put methods and the logic to 
build, parse and ignore the cached blob remains 
in BlobStoreCacheService.

Closes #78150

commit_message:
Fix TimeseriesLifecycleTypeTests.testValidateColdPhase (#79877)

This test was checking for the deprecation warning about `freeze` being
present, however, in the event that the policy is invalid due to an
invalid action, an exception is thrown before the deprecation warning
ever happens. This caused the test to fail in some cases. Resolves
#78158

commit_message:
[ML] Job updates in tests should not update blocked on deleting jobs (#78169)

Deleting jobs do not allow for their `blocked` field to be changed
from `delete`. Tests were creating job updates that ignored that
leading to a test failure when a non-noop update made no change to
a job. This commit ensures random job updates do not have a change
to the `blocked` field for deleting jobs.

Closes #78160

commit_message:
[Test] Fix test for api_key enabled (#78163)

Since #76801, the API Key feature is enabled by default and no longer
ties to HTTPS setting. This PR fixes the test to reflect the new
default.

Resolves: #78162

commit_message:
Prevent random failures in AsyncSearchActionIT size limit test (#78223)

The test lowers the response size limit to 1000 bytes, then submits an async
search. This occasionally fails because the initial response can be larger than
1000 bytes, for example if it contains any warning headers. This PR simplifies
the test to make it less fragile. We already test each response stage
individually through unit tests in AsyncSearchIndexServiceTests.

commit_message:
Fix IndexShardTests#testRestoreLocalHistoryFromTranslogOnPromotion (#79899)

Take into account the case when the promoted replica gcp is 
larger than the provided gcp.

Closes #78183
